It was around 2:15 in the afternoon on March 12th, 2024, at the construction site at 4400 Industrial Parkway. I was working on the second-floor framing when I saw the worker named Miguel step backward toward the unguarded edge of the floor opening near the elevator shaft. The opening had no guardrail and the warning tape that used to run across it had been pulled down earlier that morning when materials were moved through. He fell about fourteen feet onto the concrete slab below. He landed on his right side. He was conscious but could not stand. The site supervisor was about thirty feet away and called 911 within a minute. Paramedics arrived around 2:28. Miguel was taken to Memorial Hospital with what looked like a broken hip and a head injury. The general contractor told us to stop work for the rest of the day.
